ALL SPECIALTIES / PHARMACY TECHNICIAN / PENNSYLVANIA

Photo: Unsplash / Unsplash

Pennsylvania Pharmacy Technician Registration

Pennsylvania Pharmacy Technician license requirements, fees, and steps.

Last updated 2026-03-18 · Sources verified

  • New Applicant
  • Reciprocity
  • Renewal

New Applicant

Pharmacy Technician applications in Pennsylvania run through Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy. Use the checklist below before starting your application.

Required documents

  • Official Transcripts - Sent directly from your educational institution.
  • Exam Score Report - Official verification of passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam.

Key timeline factors

  • Board processing 4 to 6 Weeks
  • Fingerprinting Required - complete this early to avoid delays.
  • Application completeness Missing or mismatched documents are the most common reason timelines extend.

Reciprocity

Pharmacy technicians registered in another state may apply for registration in Pennsylvania by submitting a new application to the Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy. There is no interstate compact for pharmacy technicians. Applicants must provide proof of current national certification (PTCB or ExCPT), complete any Pennsylvania-specific requirements, and pass a criminal background check. Processing typically takes 4 to 6 Weeks.

  1. STEP 1

    1. Create a PALS Account

    Visit the Pennsylvania Licensing System (PALS) website at https://www.pals.pa.gov/. Click on 'New User Registration' to create an individual account. You will need to provide personal information and verify your email address to set up your User ID and password.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  2. STEP 2

    2. Access the Initial License Application

    Log in to your PALS account using your newly created credentials. From your dashboard, navigate to 'Licensing Services' and select 'Apply for an Initial License'. From the dropdown menu, choose 'Pharmacy Technician' as the specific license type you are applying for.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  3. STEP 3

    3. Complete the Online Application Form

    Carefully complete all sections of the online Pharmacy Technician application. This includes providing personal details, educational background (high school diploma or GED equivalent), and answering questions regarding your criminal history and professional conduct. Ensure all information is accurate and complete.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  4. STEP 4

    4. Submit Required Supporting Documentation

    Electronically upload any necessary supporting documents as prompted by the PALS system. This typically includes a current criminal history record check (CHRC) from the Pennsylvania State Police (or other approved sources if applicable) and any other proof of eligibility as specified by the Board. Ensure documents meet format and size requirements.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  5. STEP 5

    5. Pay the Application Fee

    Proceed to the payment section within the PALS system to remit the non-refundable application fee. The fee for an initial Pharmacy Technician license is $30.00. Payment can be made using a credit or debit card.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

  6. STEP 6

    6. Monitor Application Status and Await Approval

    After submitting your application and fee, you can monitor the status of your application by logging into your PALS account. The Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy will review your submission. Once approved, your Pharmacy Technician license will be issued and reflected in your PALS profile.

    Fee: $30 · Timeline: 4 to 6 Weeks

Fees and Costs

Application fee
$30
Background check
See board if applicable
Jurisprudence exam
$0
Total estimated cost
$30

Always confirm current fees on the board site before submitting.

Renewal

Pharmacy Technician renewal in Pennsylvania follows the board cycle below.

Renewal cycle
Every 2 years
Renewal fee
$70
Continuing education
See board

Frequently Asked Questions

How much does a Pennsylvania pharmacy technician license cost?

The Pennsylvania State Board of Pharmacy snapshot lists a $30 initial pharmacy technician registration fee and a $70 biennial renewal fee.

Is national certification required?

Yes, passing the PTCB (Pharmacy Technician Certification Board) or ExCPT exam is a standard requirement for initial licensure across almost all states.

Does Pennsylvania have reciprocity for pharmacy technicians?

No interstate compact currently covers pharmacy technician practice in Pennsylvania. To work in Pennsylvania, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my pharmacy technician license to Pennsylvania?

Pennsylvania does not publish a pharmacy technician processing timeline on its public registration snapshot page. Check the board portal for status updates.

Can I start working in Pennsylvania while my endorsement application is processing?

Contact the Pennsylvania Board of Pharmacy to determine whether a temporary or trainee permit is available while your endorsement application is being processed. Requirements vary, and some applicants may need to wait for full approval before beginning work in Pennsylvania.

At a glance

Endorsement fee

$30

Processing time

4 to 6 Weeks

Fingerprinting

Required

Jurisprudence exam

Not required

Temporary license

Available · See board

National exam

See board

Compact

No · plan on endorsement

Renewal

Every 2 years · $70

Licensing board
